_meta_project_goal: Render the complete CauseFlow product and API documentation site from MDX through Mintlify, with every page reachable from navigation, zero broken links, valid frontmatter, and machine-verifiable content invariants holding across all 133 `.mdx` files.
_meta_note: Top-level project_goal and mode are embedded here as _meta_* fields because the harness reconcile.mjs requires the JSON root to be a flat array of Work Items. The two meta fields are documentary; the array root remains the source of truth for the generator.
description: Audit: `mint dev` from the project root serves `http://localhost:3000/` with HTTP 200, the site name 'CauseFlow AI', and the Quickstart intro card.
steps: Step 1: from the project root (directory containing `docs.json`), start `mint dev` in the background on port 3000 (or $PORT).
Step 2: wait for `GET http://localhost:3000/` to return HTTP 200.
Step 3: verify the response body contains the site name 'CauseFlow AI' and the intro card labelled 'Quickstart'; stop the dev server and mark the audit as passing only if both appear.
verify_notes: VERIFY-FIRST pass at real HTTP boundary: `mint dev --port 3000` from project root served http://localhost:3000/ with HTTP 200; response body contains <title>CauseFlow AI Documentation - CauseFlow AI</title> and the Quickstart intro Card (href=/getting-started/quickstart, title=Quickstart). Zero code diff (existing-codebase checkpoint).

description: Audit: `mint broken-links` from the project root exits 0 and reports zero broken links across the site.
steps: Step 1: from the project root, run `mint broken-links`.
Step 2: verify exit code is 0.
Step 3: verify stdout/stderr reports zero broken links; the audit passes only on a clean run.
verify_notes: VERIFY-FIRST pass at real boundary: `mint broken-links` from project root exited 0 with 'success no broken links found'. Root cause of prior failure: harness artifact dirs (plans/, tasks/, docs/, harness-progress/) contain .md planning/spec files with non-backtick JSX-like tokens (<40, <Note>, <ParamField>, <Integration Name>) that broke MDX parsing; these are not docs pages (none in docs.json navigation). Fix: added plans/, tasks/, docs/, harness-progress/, session-learnings.md, INVARIANTS.md, CLAUDE.md to .mintignore. Diff = .mintignore only; no docs source/content changed.

description: Audit: `docs.json` is valid JSON, validates against the Mintlify `docs.json` schema, and every navigation `pages` entry resolves to a real `.mdx` file under the project root.
steps: Step 1: validate `docs.json` parses as JSON.
Step 2: validate `docs.json` against `https://mintlify.com/docs.json` (or run `mint dev` and confirm no schema/parse error).
Step 3: walk every `tab` -> `group` -> `pages` entry and confirm the referenced file exists as `.mdx` under the project root; the audit passes only if every page resolves.

description: Audit: every `.mdx` (excluding `.mintlify/`, `.artifacts/`, `node_modules/`, `drafts/`) has YAML frontmatter with both `title` and `description` fields.
steps: Step 1: walk every `.mdx` under the project root minus the excluded directories.
Step 2: confirm each file's frontmatter block contains `title:` and `description:` keys.
Step 3: a scripted `find … head -5 … grep` reports zero `MISSING TITLE: …` lines; the audit passes only if every file is compliant.

description: Audit: every `description` field in `.mdx` frontmatter is at most 160 characters.
steps: Step 1: parse the frontmatter `description` value from every `.mdx`.
Step 2: run `awk`-style length check on the value of each `description`.
Step 3: confirm no value exceeds 160 characters; the audit passes only if all descriptions fit.
verify_notes: Integrated Verification passed on main. 133 MDX files audited; zero descriptions exceed 160 chars (max=153). HTTP boundary confirmed on PORT=5170.

description: Audit: all four navigation tabs (Documentation, API reference, Relay, Changelog) render in `mint dev`, and the Changelog tab reaches a page whose H1 matches the frontmatter `title` of `changelog/index.mdx`.
steps: Step 1: start `mint dev` and load each of the four tabs in the sidebar.
Step 2: open the Changelog tab and load the index page.
Step 3: confirm the rendered H1 equals the `title` field in `changelog/index.mdx`; the audit passes only if all four tabs render and the H1 matches.

description: Audit: the `docs.json` redirect `{"source": "/quickstart", "destination": "/getting-started/quickstart"}` resolves at runtime — `GET /quickstart` returns 200 and lands on the Quickstart page.
steps: Step 1: start `mint dev`.
Step 2: `GET /quickstart` and confirm HTTP 200.
Step 3: confirm the response body is the Quickstart page (H1 matches the Quickstart frontmatter `title`); the audit passes only if the redirect resolves cleanly.

description: Audit: each of the seven Getting-started pages (Quickstart, Key concepts, How it works, AI transparency, Skills, Memory and chat, Triggers) is reachable from the documentation tab in `mint dev` and renders without MDX parse errors.
steps: Step 1: list the seven pages from `docs.json` under the Getting-started group.
Step 2: load each page in `mint dev` and confirm the response is HTTP 200 with no MDX parse error in the dev log.
Step 3: the audit passes only if all seven pages render cleanly.

description: Audit: each of the seven Dashboard pages (Overview, Incidents, Analyses, Remediations, Audit trail, Team management, Settings) is reachable from the documentation tab and renders without MDX parse errors.
steps: Step 1: list the seven pages from `docs.json` under the Dashboard group.
Step 2: load each page in `mint dev` and confirm HTTP 200 with no MDX parse error in the dev log.
Step 3: the audit passes only if all seven pages render cleanly.

description: Audit: each of the nine Integrations pages (Overview, Monitoring, GitHub, Communication, Project management, Databases, Custom webhooks, Cloud providers, HubSpot) is reachable from the documentation tab and renders without MDX parse errors.
steps: Step 1: list the nine pages from `docs.json` under the Integrations group.
Step 2: load each page in `mint dev` and confirm HTTP 200 with no MDX parse error in the dev log.
Step 3: the audit passes only if all nine pages render cleanly.

description: Audit: each of the three Billing pages (Plans, Usage and credits, Manage subscription), the five Security pages (Overview, Authentication, RBAC, Data privacy, Compliance), and the Changelog index is reachable and renders without MDX parse errors.
steps: Step 1: list the three Billing pages, five Security pages, and the Changelog index from `docs.json`.
Step 2: load each page in `mint dev` and confirm HTTP 200 with no MDX parse error in the dev log.
Step 3: the audit passes only if all nine pages render cleanly.

description: Audit: the API reference introduction page renders with H1 'API introduction', a code block showing exactly `https://api.causeflow.ai`, and the version note 'v1' — `mint dev` does not log a parse error for `api-reference/introduction.mdx`.
steps: Step 1: load `api-reference/introduction.mdx` in `mint dev`.
Step 2: confirm H1 is 'API introduction'.
Step 3: confirm the body contains the exact base URL `https://api.causeflow.ai` and the 'v1' version note, and the dev log has no parse error for that file.

description: Audit: every endpoint page listed under the API reference tab in `docs.json` (incidents, triage, investigation, remediation, memory, skills, triggers, integrations, knowledge, graph, billing, notifications, audit, webhooks, GitHub, tenants, API keys, analytics, health, widget, relay) renders with H1 matching its frontmatter `title`.
steps: Step 1: list every page under the API reference tab in `docs.json`.
Step 2: load each page in `mint dev` (or curl its rendered URL) and read the H1.
Step 3: confirm the rendered H1 matches the `title` field of that page's frontmatter; the audit passes only if every page matches.

description: Audit: the Authentication page renders sections for JWT Bearer, API keys, and Webhook HMAC signature verification with working code examples, and the `verifyWebhookSignature` snippet passes `node --check`.
steps: Step 1: load `api-reference/authentication.mdx` in `mint dev`.
Step 2: confirm sections for JWT Bearer, API keys, and HMAC verification are present with code examples.
Step 3: extract the `verifyWebhookSignature` snippet into a temp file and run `node --check` on it; the audit passes only if it parses.
verify_notes: VERIFY-FIRST PASS: mint dev on port 5174 — GET /api-reference/authentication returns 200; rendered Playwright-captured body contains all three sections (JWT Bearer token with h2, curl + TypeScript examples; API key authentication with X-API-Key header example; Webhook HMAC signature with h2, bash + TypeScript examples including verifyWebhookSignature function); verifyWebhookSignature function passes node --check; Bash HMAC example produces correct HMAC (cross-verified with Node.js crypto.createHmac); verifyWebhookSignature logic end-to-end (valid, invalid, empty body) all PASS. Zero defects. Feature_list.json status corrected from stale false to true.

description: Audit: the Errors and pagination page renders the JSON error envelope example, the HTTP status-code table (400/401/403/404/409/429/500/503), and a cursor-pagination example with `items`, `cursor`, and `count` fields.
steps: Step 1: load the Errors and pagination page in `mint dev`.
Step 2: confirm a JSON error envelope example is rendered.
Step 3: confirm the status-code table lists 400/401/403/404/409/429/500/503, and a cursor-pagination example shows `items`, `cursor`, and `count`.
verify_notes: INTEGRATED VERIFICATION PASS 2026-07-09: required scaffold exists (133 MDX files and required top-level directories); existing Mintlify preview on port 5174 returned HTTP 200 for / and /api-reference/errors-and-pagination. Rendered target page contains the JSON error envelope, all required status codes (400/401/403/404/409/429/500/503), and cursor-pagination fields items, cursor, and count. Evidence: .harness-evidence/api-reference/WI-AC-015-2026-07-09-errors-and-pagination-rendered.html. Zero defects.

description: Audit: every API example URL across the api-reference pages uses the host `https://api.causeflow.ai` only — `grep -rE 'api\.causeflow\.(io|dev|local|prod)' --include='*.mdx'` returns no matches.
steps: Step 1: run `grep -rE 'api\.causeflow\.(io|dev|local|prod)' --include='*.mdx'` over the project root.
Step 2: confirm zero matches.
Step 3: spot-check a sample of api-reference pages to confirm every example URL uses the `https://api.causeflow.ai` host.
verify_notes: Independent QA PASS 2026-07-09: project_specs.xml read and required scaffold present; 133 MDX files total, 84 under api-reference. Exact AC-016 command `grep -rE 'api\.causeflow\.(io|dev|local|prod)' --include='*.mdx' .` exited 1 with no output, confirming zero forbidden CauseFlow API hosts. Zero defects.

description: Audit: every tenant-ID placeholder matches `ten_EXAMPLE_…`, every API-key placeholder matches `cflo_live_sk_EXAMPLE_…`, and no real-looking UUIDs or full JWTs appear in any example.
steps: Step 1: run `grep -rE '(ten_[a-zA-Z0-9]{16,})|(cflo_live_sk_[a-zA-Z0-9]{16,})' --include='*.mdx' | grep -v EXAMPLE`.
Step 2: confirm zero matches (only `ten_EXAMPLE_…` and `cflo_live_sk_EXAMPLE_…` should remain after the `EXAMPLE` filter).
Step 3: confirm no full JWTs (e.g. `eyJ…longbody…`) appear in any example; the audit passes only if all placeholders follow the approved shape.

description: Audit: outbound events catalog (`api-reference/webhooks/outbound-events.mdx`) renders a table with exactly 20 distinct dot-namespaced events, and the introduction page's event count is reconciled to 20 (the event catalog is the single source of truth).
steps: Step 1: open `api-reference/webhooks/outbound-events.mdx` and count the dot-namespaced event rows in the catalog table.
Step 2: confirm the count is exactly 20.
Step 3: open `api-reference/introduction.mdx` and confirm its stated event count is also 20 (or it links to outbound-events.mdx as the source of truth); the audit passes only when both numbers align.

description: Audit: the Relay tab renders each of its eight pages (Overview, Architecture, Quickstart, Configuration, PII masking, Policy engine, Deployment, Troubleshooting) without MDX parse errors, and the Overview page serves the Mermaid `flowchart TD` block as a rendered diagram (not raw code).
steps: Step 1: list the eight pages from `docs.json` under the Relay tab.
Step 2: load each page in `mint dev` and confirm HTTP 200 with no MDX parse error.

Step 3: load `relay/overview.mdx` and confirm the Mermaid `flowchart TD` block is served as inline SVG / rendered diagram, not raw text; the audit passes only on full coverage plus a rendered diagram.

description: Audit: the Relay Configuration page documents `controlPlane`, `resources`, `allowedOperations`, `maxRowsPerQuery`, and the `${VAR_NAME}` env-var substitution — `grep` for each key returns at least one match in `relay/configuration.mdx`.
steps: Step 1: `grep -E 'controlPlane' relay/configuration.mdx` returns ≥ 1 match.
Step 2: `grep -E 'resources|allowedOperations|maxRowsPerQuery' relay/configuration.mdx` returns ≥ 1 match each.
Step 3: `grep -E '\$\{[A-Z_]+\}' relay/configuration.mdx` returns ≥ 1 match (the `${VAR_NAME}` substitution pattern); the audit passes only if all five required keys/patterns are documented.

description: Audit: the Relay Overview's 'What the Relay is not' section names proxy, tunnel, and replication-agent anti-patterns — `grep -E 'Not a (proxy|tunnel)|replication agent' relay/overview.mdx` returns all three matches.
steps: Step 1: load `relay/overview.mdx` and locate the 'What the Relay is not' section.
Step 2: `grep -E 'Not a (proxy|tunnel)|replication agent' relay/overview.mdx` returns three matches.
Step 3: the audit passes only if the section names all three anti-patterns.
verify_notes: Integrated Verification passed on main. Required scaffold present; `mint dev --port 5170` served `/relay/overview` with HTTP 200; rendered HTML contains 'What the Relay is not', 'Not a proxy', 'Not a tunnel', and 'Not a replication agent'; source grep returned exactly three matches.

description: Audit: severity tokens across every `.mdx` are restricted to `critical | high | medium | low | info` — `grep -rEn 'severity[: ].*(emergency|urgent|notice|debug|warn)' --include='*.mdx'` returns no matches.
steps: Step 1: run `grep -rEn 'severity[: ].*(emergency|urgent|notice|debug|warn)' --include='*.mdx'` over the project root.
Step 2: confirm zero matches.
Step 3: spot-check a few `.mdx` files that reference severity to confirm only the approved tokens (`critical`, `high`, `medium`, `low`, `info`) appear.

description: Audit: no `Incident.status` value of `dismissed` or `failed` appears in any `.mdx`; `pending` appears only for step-level or approval-level status.
steps: Step 1: run `grep -rEn '"status": *"(dismissed|failed)"' --include='*.mdx'` over the project root.
Step 2: confirm zero matches for `dismissed` or `failed`.
Step 3: confirm every `pending` occurrence is in a step/approval context, not an `Incident.status`; the audit passes only if the lifecycle enum is respected.
verify_notes: Integrated Verification passed on main. Required scaffold present with 133 MDX files; AC-023 grep for dismissed/failed JSON statuses returned zero matches; pending occurrences are step-level or approval-level/invite text, not Incident.status; check-invariants.sh --quiet passed; mint dev --port 5180 served / and /api-reference/remediation/list-remediations with HTTP 200 and no parse markers.

description: Audit: no `.mdx` references AWS ARNs, `.internal` hostnames, `sqs.<region>.amazonaws.com` URLs, 12-digit account IDs adjacent to 'account'/'arn', KMS key IDs, LangFuse/Hindsight URLs, internal DynamoDB table names, or ECS cluster/task-definition names.
steps: Step 1: run the audit grep `grep -rEi '(arn:aws:|\.internal[^a-z]|sqs\.[a-z0-9-]+\.amazonaws\.com|[0-9]{12}.*(account|arn)|kms:key/[a-f0-9-]{36}|langfuse|hindsight\.[a-z]+|causeflow-(staging|production)[- ]|/ecs/causeflow)' --include='*.mdx'`.
Step 2: confirm zero matches across all 133 `.mdx` files.
Step 3: spot-check `INVARIANTS.md` aligns with the regex; the audit passes only if zero matches return and `INVARIANTS.md` documents the boundary.
verify_notes: Integrated Verification PASS 2026-07-09: AC-024 audit grep returned zero matches across 133 MDX files for all forbidden patterns (AWS ARNs, .internal hostnames, SQS URLs, 12-digit account IDs, KMS key IDs, LangFuse URLs, Hindsight URLs, internal DynamoDB table names, ECS cluster/task-definition names); check-invariants.sh --quiet exit 0. Zero defects.

description: Audit: RBAC role names across all MDX are restricted to `admin` and `member`; no other role tokens appear in RBAC context.
steps: Step 1: run `grep -rn 'roles\?:' --include='*.mdx'` and inspect every `roles:` / `role:` line.
Step 2: confirm the only role names listed in any RBAC context are `admin` and `member`.
Step 3: the audit passes only if no third role token (e.g. `owner`, `viewer`, `editor`) appears under any `roles` or `role:` line.

description: From a clean checkout with a blank shell env (no `MINTLIFY_*`, `MINTLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN`, `MINTLIFY_DEPLOY_TOKEN`, `CLERK_*`, `STRIPE_*`, `AWS_*`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `SENTRY_*`, `LANGFUSE_*`, `SVIX_*`, `SLACK_*`, or `COMPOSIO_*` set), `docker compose up -d` from the project root brings up the `causeflow-docs` service on port 3000. Within 60 seconds `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3000/` returns 200 and the response body contains the site name "CauseFlow AI" and the intro card "Quickstart". `docker logs causeflow-docs 2>&1 | grep -E 'mintlify\.com|mintlify\.app|clerk\.com|stripe\.com|amazonaws\.com|anthropic\.com|claude\.ai|openai\.com|chatgpt\.com|sentry\.io|langfuse\.io|svix\.com|slack\.com|composio\.dev'` returns zero matches.
steps: Verify AC-026: From a clean checkout with a blank shell env (no `MINTLIFY_*`, `MINTLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN`, `MINTLIFY_DEPLOY_TOKEN`, `CLERK_*`, `STRIPE_*`, `AWS_*`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `SENTRY_*`, `LANGFUSE_*`, `SVIX_*`, `SLACK_*`, or `COMPOSIO_*` set), `docker compose up -d` from the project root brings up the `causeflow-docs` service on port 3000. Within 60 seconds `curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3000/` returns 200 and the response body contains the site name "CauseFlow AI" and the intro card "Quickstart". `docker logs causeflow-docs 2>&1 | grep -E 'mintlify\.com|mintlify\.app|clerk\.com|stripe\.com|amazonaws\.com|anthropic\.com|claude\.ai|openai\.com|chatgpt\.com|sentry\.io|langfuse\.io|svix\.com|slack\.com|composio\.dev'` returns zero matches.

description: The project ships a multi-stage `Dockerfile` at the project root. The `builder` stage installs Node.js and the `mint` CLI (Mintlify's open-source CLI) and produces a built site (static export or a runnable dev server bundle). The `runtime` stage copies the built site into a minimal image — `nginx:alpine` for the static variant, or `node:22-alpine` for the dev-server variant. The runtime image contains no `.env`, no `MINTLIFY_*` env vars, and no Mintlify account credentials. `docker build . -t causeflow-docs:test` exits 0; for the nginx variant the resulting image is under 200 MB (no `node_modules` carried into the runtime stage). `docker inspect causeflow-docs:test` shows the runtime image has no entrypoint that talks to `mintlify.com` or any other external SaaS host.
steps: Verify AC-027: The project ships a multi-stage `Dockerfile` at the project root. The `builder` stage installs Node.js and the `mint` CLI (Mintlify's open-source CLI) and produces a built site (static export or a runnable dev server bundle). The `runtime` stage copies the built site into a minimal image — `nginx:alpine` for the static variant, or `node:22-alpine` for the dev-server variant. The runtime image contains no `.env`, no `MINTLIFY_*` env vars, and no Mintlify account credentials. `docker build . -t causeflow-docs:test` exits 0; for the nginx variant the resulting image is under 200 MB (no `node_modules` carried into the runtime stage). `docker inspect causeflow-docs:test` shows the runtime image has no entrypoint that talks to `mintlify.com` or any other external SaaS host.

description: `docs.json#contextual.options` no longer contains `claude` or `chatgpt`. After a reader invokes the contextual menu on any page (for example via Playwright clicking the per-page "contextual" trigger), no outbound request is made to `anthropic.com`, `claude.ai`, `openai.com`, or `chatgpt.com` (verifiable via Playwright network logging). The contextual menu only offers local actions (`copy` and `view`) — the AI-provider delegate that the Mintlify contextual feature wired to those SaaS endpoints is fully removed from the runtime path. `grep -E '"(claude|chatgpt)"' docs.json` returns zero matches.
steps: Verify AC-028: `docs.json#contextual.options` no longer contains `claude` or `chatgpt`. After a reader invokes the contextual menu on any page (for example via Playwright clicking the per-page "contextual" trigger), no outbound request is made to `anthropic.com`, `claude.ai`, `openai.com`, or `chatgpt.com` (verifiable via Playwright network logging). The contextual menu only offers local actions (`copy` and `view`) — the AI-provider delegate that the Mintlify contextual feature wired to those SaaS endpoints is fully removed from the runtime path. `grep -E '"(claude|chatgpt)"' docs.json` returns zero matches.

description: No Mintlify hosting SaaS. `grep -rE 'mintlify\.com|mintlify\.app|mintlify deploy|mintlify auth' --include='*.{json,mdx,yml,yaml,sh,Dockerfile,ts,js,mjs,md}'` returns no matches in the project. The Dockerfile does not invoke `mintlify deploy` or `mint deploy` and does not require a Mintlify account token. `README.md` documents `docker compose up` as the canonical run path; the prior "Changes pushed to the default branch are deployed automatically via the Mintlify GitHub integration" sentence is removed. No GitHub Actions workflow in the project runs `mint deploy` or `mintlify deploy`.
steps: Verify AC-029: No Mintlify hosting SaaS. `grep -rE 'mintlify\.com|mintlify\.app|mintlify deploy|mintlify auth' --include='*.{json,mdx,yml,yaml,sh,Dockerfile,ts,js,mjs,md}'` returns no matches in the project. The Dockerfile does not invoke `mintlify deploy` or `mint deploy` and does not require a Mintlify account token. `README.md` documents `docker compose up` as the canonical run path; the prior "Changes pushed to the default branch are deployed automatically via the Mintlify GitHub integration" sentence is removed. No GitHub Actions workflow in the project runs `mint deploy` or `mintlify deploy`.

description: The project's `.env.example` is either absent or contains only optional `MINTLIFY_*` overrides (e.g. a port override). The container's required env vars are minimal: `PORT` (default 3000) for the runtime stage. No `MINTLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN`, `MINTLIFY_DEPLOY_TOKEN`, `MINTLIFY_DEPLOYMENT_ID`, `CLERK_*`, `STRIPE_*`, `AWS_*`, `SENTRY_*`, `LANGFUSE_*`, `SVIX_*`, `SLACK_*`, or `COMPOSIO_*` env vars are referenced anywhere in `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`, or `docs.json`. `grep -E 'CLERK_|STRIPE_|AWS_|SENTRY_|LANGFUSE_|SVIX_|SLACK_|COMPOSIO_|MINTLIFY_(AUTH|DEPLOY)' .env.example Dockerfile docker-compose.yml` returns zero matches.
steps: Verify AC-030: The project's `.env.example` is either absent or contains only optional `MINTLIFY_*` overrides (e.g. a port override). The container's required env vars are minimal: `PORT` (default 3000) for the runtime stage. No `MINTLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN`, `MINTLIFY_DEPLOY_TOKEN`, `MINTLIFY_DEPLOYMENT_ID`, `CLERK_*`, `STRIPE_*`, `AWS_*`, `SENTRY_*`, `LANGFUSE_*`, `SVIX_*`, `SLACK_*`, or `COMPOSIO_*` env vars are referenced anywhere in `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`, or `docs.json`. `grep -E 'CLERK_|STRIPE_|AWS_|SENTRY_|LANGFUSE_|SVIX_|SLACK_|COMPOSIO_|MINTLIFY_(AUTH|DEPLOY)' .env.example Dockerfile docker-compose.yml` returns zero matches.

description: Regression: all 25 original acceptance checks (AC-001 through AC-025) pass against the docker-compose stack. Specifically: `curl http://localhost:3000/` returns 200 with the homepage content; `GET /quickstart` returns 200 and lands on the Quickstart page (the redirect from `docs.json#redirects` still resolves); every navigation tab (Documentation, API reference, Relay, Changelog) renders; the Mermaid diagram on the Relay overview renders as SVG (not raw code); the Authentication page covers JWT Bearer, API key, and HMAC verification; the Outbound events catalog still lists 20 events; the content invariants from AC-022 through AC-025 (severity enum, status enum, no AWS ARNs / `.internal` / SQS URLs / LangFuse URLs / Hindsight URLs / KMS IDs, RBAC role enum) still hold across all 133 MDX files. `mint broken-links` against the source tree still exits 0 with zero broken links.
steps: Verify AC-031: Regression: all 25 original acceptance checks (AC-001 through AC-025) pass against the docker-compose stack. Specifically: `curl http://localhost:3000/` returns 200 with the homepage content; `GET /quickstart` returns 200 and lands on the Quickstart page (the redirect from `docs.json#redirects` still resolves); every navigation tab (Documentation, API reference, Relay, Changelog) renders; the Mermaid diagram on the Relay overview renders as SVG (not raw code); the Authentication page covers JWT Bearer, API key, and HMAC verification; the Outbound events catalog still lists 20 events; the content invariants from AC-022 through AC-025 (severity enum, status enum, no AWS ARNs / `.internal` / SQS URLs / LangFuse URLs / Hindsight URLs / KMS IDs, RBAC role enum) still hold across all 133 MDX files. `mint broken-links` against the source tree still exits 0 with zero broken links.
verify_notes: REGRESSION PASS on docker-compose stack (PORT=5179, clean rebuild). Fixed two defects: (1) export serve.js ignored docs.json#redirects and Mintlify <dir>/index/index.html layout -> /quickstart (redirect) and /changelog (tab landing) 404'd; added repo-root serve-docs.js (pure Node, PORT only) wired via Dockerfile runtime stage (COPY docs.json + serve-docs.js as serve.js) so redirects resolve as 200 internal rewrites and directory-index pages serve. (2) AC-024 AWS-ARN invariant violated across 8 MDX (19 lines incl. real 12-digit account id 409171461008) -> replaced all arn:aws: literals with compliant placeholder tokens per INVARIANTS.md. Added frontmatter to 2 orphaned snippets so AC-004 holds. Verified: / ->200 CauseFlow AI+Quickstart; /quickstart ->200 lands Quickstart; /changelog ->200; 4 tabs render; Relay Mermaid via Mermaid component (no raw <pre>); Auth JWT/API-key/HMAC; 20 events; all 82 API ref pages + 125 nav pages 200; mint broken-links exit 0; AC-022/23/24/25, AC-016/17 grep zero; AC-004/005 frontmatter ok; 133 MDX. forbidden-host log grep=0.

description: The built site, when served by the runtime image, renders the same 133 MDX pages, the same four navigation tabs, the same redirect (`/quickstart` → `/getting-started/quickstart`), and the same Mintlify components (Card, CardGroup, Steps, Note, Tip, Warning, Info, CodeGroup, ParamField, Tabs, Mermaid) without MDX parse errors. Every endpoint page listed under the API reference tab (incidents, triage, investigation, remediation, memory, skills, triggers, integrations, knowledge, graph, billing, notifications, audit, webhooks, GitHub, tenants, API keys, analytics, health, widget, relay) renders its H1 matching the `title` frontmatter. The Dockerfile's runtime stage is reproducible: rebuilding from a clean Docker cache produces a functionally identical image that passes AC-031.
steps: Verify AC-032: The built site, when served by the runtime image, renders the same 133 MDX pages, the same four navigation tabs, the same redirect (`/quickstart` → `/getting-started/quickstart`), and the same Mintlify components (Card, CardGroup, Steps, Note, Tip, Warning, Info, CodeGroup, ParamField, Tabs, Mermaid) without MDX parse errors. description: The `docker-compose.yml` ships with only the `causeflow-docs` service (port 3000). It does not reference any AWS, Stripe, Clerk, Sentry, Langfuse, Svix, Slack, Composio, or Mintlify SaaS endpoint. The compose file is valid (`docker compose config` exits 0) and the service depends on no other service. When the monorepo's root `docker-compose.yml` (defined by the `core` / `web` open-source-local-runtime contexts) includes the `public-docs` service as well, the docs site is reachable at `http://localhost:3000/` with no additional env vars and no other services needing to be up first; the docs service starts in parallel with the other subprojects' services.

verify_notes: VERIFY-FIRST (AC-033). No source changes required: the `docker-compose.yml` shipped in WI-AC-026 already satisfies every clause. Black-box verification on assigned port 5179 (host 3000 occupied by relay-control-plane-stub; ran the built `causeflow-docs:local` image as `causeflow-docs-ac033` with `-p 5179:3000 -e PORT=3000`; canonical compose stays `3000:3000`). Clauses: (1) `docker compose config --services` returns only `causeflow-docs`, ports `3000:3000`; (2) `grep -Eni 'aws|stripe|clerk|sentry|langfuse|svix|slack|composio|mintlify|amazonaws|anthropic|openai|chatgpt|claude' docker-compose.yml` -> 0 matches (no SaaS endpoints); (3) `docker compose config` exits 0; (4) resolved compose config has no `depends_on`/`links`/`external_links`/`healthcheck` -> service depends on no other service; (5) portable: runtime `Env` is just `PORT=3000` (+ base-image PATH/NODE_VERSION/YARN_VERSION), build context is local, no external networks, no healthcheck dependency -> the service is self-contained, requires no additional env vars, and starts in parallel with other subprojects' services when the monorepo root compose (owned by core/web contexts WI-AC-039/WI-AC-044) includes `public-docs`. Live: `GET http://localhost:5179/` -> 200, body has `CauseFlow AI` (x4) and `Quickstart` (x3); boot log `Serving docs at http://localhost:3000`; `docker logs | grep -cE` forbidden-host pattern (mintlify.com|...|composio.dev) -> 0.
